title: Rak'qua

{{Quote|Not far from the Tansarii Point Station which we are on, there's another outpost, called Gamma. On it live a tribe of xenomorphs called the Rak'qua.|[[Antom Moria]], to an officer|<ref name="SWG:OA">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Officer Antom|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
The '''Rak'qua''' were a bellicose tribe of [[Nautolan/Legends|Nautolans]] who lived before and during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Description==
[[File:RakquaAphotic-SWG.png|thumb|left|150px|A Rak'qua Aphotic.]]
{{Quote|You should be mindful of the Rak'qua Overlord. There are other powerful denizens within Station Gamma, as well. My sensors have picked up other mechanical signals in the area. You should be careful.|[[E-5KL]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:TSG2">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|To Station Gamma!|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
The Rak'qua were a Nautolan tribe<ref name="SWG:SG" /> composed mostly of warriors, and led by an [[overlord]]. They formed a religious group and also counted a number of [[Shaman/Legends|shamans]].<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> The beliefs of the Rak'qua involved prayers and dances around altars, hoping that they would bring them protection and prowess in battle.<ref name="SWG:AR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Aquillia's Runes|Station Gamma}}</ref> The Rak'qua had a strong sense of honor.<ref name="SWG:AMS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Antom's Missing Student|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

The Rak'qua were known to use [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], including [[I-10 Probe Droid]]s, [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]], [[K3-I Buzzer Droid]]s, [[I-8 Black Widow]]s, [[IT-4 Warning Droid]]s, [[ESPY E.R. Droid]]s, [[I-7 Construct]]s and [[rescind E.R. Droid]]s. They also trained [[merek]]s as war beasts.<ref name="SWG:SG2" />

==History==
{{Quote|Station Gamma was a research facility. Now it's run by an Overlord and his cronies.|[[Frelka]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:F">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Frelka|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
Years priors to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the Rak'qua arrived in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]] and overran [[Station Gamma]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] which was used as a research station by [[Jorj Car'das]]' [[Car'das/Legends|organization]].<ref name="SWG:SG" /> They turned the station into a stronghold that was defended by a large number of Rak'qua warriors, battle droids and war beasts, including mereks and a large creature named [[Ageaughu]].<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> Although all the former inhabitants of the station were wiped out during the takeover,<ref name="SWG:SG" /> no conflict erupted between the Rak'qua and the Car'das from neighboring [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]] and cohabitation between the two stations remained quite peaceful.<ref name="SWG:LtS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|Lercer the Spy|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Moreover, the Rak'qua made an arrangement with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in order to strengthen their position, therefore allowing the Empire to install a communication system in Station Gamma.<ref name="SWG:GIFSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Imperial|Get Information From Station Gamma|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

[[File:TwilekVsRakqua-SWG.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A [[Unidentified genetically altered Twi'lek|genetically altered Twi'lek]] fighting Rak'qua warriors on Station Gamma.]]
After the Battle of Yavin, the hostility between Station Gamma and Tansarii Point Station finally turned to open conflict.<ref name="SWG:LtS" /> The warfare essentially erupted because a greedy arms dealer based in the Bright Jewel system bribed people on both sides to take advantage of a conflict in order to sell weapons.<ref name="SWG:TSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Spy|The Spy's Game|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> The Car'das dispatched soldiers from Tansarii Point Station to fight the Rak'qua on Station Gamma.<ref name="SWG:MS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Missing Soldier|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> At that time, the Rak'qua were led by Overlord [[Rrynt'vor]].<ref name="SWG:SG2" />

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> groups of spacers allied with the Car'das,<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> including a [[Unidentified genetically altered Twi'lek|genetically altered Twi'lek]],<ref name="MoGS">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?page=Mystery%20on%20Gamma%20Station&resource=features|text=Mystery on Gamma Station}}</ref> joined the fight on Station Gamma<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> and the Rak'qua suffered heavy casualties.<ref name="SWG:AMS" /> The [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Lercer Gaia]] later discovered the involvement of the arms dealer in the outbreak of the conflict.<ref name="SWG:TSG" /> With this information, [[Officer/Legends|Officer]] [[Antom Moria]] was finally able to convinced both parties that the war was meaningless and prevented more violence.<ref name="SWG:PaW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Officer|Preventing a War|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

In the meantime, the Rak'qua broke their agreement with the Empire and put a lock on the Imperial communication system set on Station Gamma. Concerned by the situation, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] dispatched [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Agent]] [[Falrey]] to Tansarii Point Station. The latter recruited a group of loyal Imperial spacers who infiltrated Station Gamma to find information about the Rak'qua treachery.<ref name="SWG:GIFSG">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Imperial|Get Information From Station Gamma|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Rak'qua appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:SG2" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The Rak'qua were added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]]," introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>
